summary: 07/27/1996: At 1:25 Saturday morning, a pipe bomb detonated in Centennial Olympic Park in Atlanta, Georgia, United States. At the time of the explosion there was a crowd of approximately 60,000 people in the park attending a concert and festivities in celebration of the 1996 Olympic Games. One person was killed as a direct result of the explosion and 111 were injured, including six state troopers and one Georgia Bureau of Investigation agent. A cameraman suffered a heart attack and died while running toward the scene. A call to 911 from a nearby phone booth warned There is bomb in Centennial Park. You have 30 minutes. However, there were delays in communicating this message to authorities in the park, where the bomb was found about five minutes before it exploded. Analysts suspect that the call was intended to lure law enforcement officials to the park to injure them. Authorities initially and erroneously suspected that the person who found the bomb under a bench in the park and warned authorities about it was responsible for planting the device. In 2005, Eric Rudolph was convicted and sentenced to four life terms for carrying out this attack and others, including the bombing of an abortion clinic in January 1997 and the bombing of a gay and lesbian bar in February 1997.
motive: Rudolph indicated he wanted to force the cancellation of the Olympic Games and confound, anger and embarrass the federal government for sanctioning abortion. In a letter, Rudolph threatened to target all facilities and personnel of the federal government and used language consistent with Christian Identity, a white supremacist movement. Analysts suspected that the 911 call in advance of the explosion was intended to lure law enforcement officials to the site and harm them.

motive: To protest the IRS and the taxation system of the United States government

summary: 05/07/2002: Between the dates of May 3-7, 2002, eighteen pipe bombs were found in rural mailboxes in five Midwestern states in the U.S.A., collectively causing seven injuries and leading to widespread panic in the region. Most of the explosives came with typewritten notes that bemoaned the power of the government and threatened more attacks. In most incidents, the bomb did not detonate and no injuries were suffered. In Iowa, six of the seven bombs detonated, injuring four mail carriers and two residents. Each of the bombs had a note in a plastic bag, which stated, I'm obtaining your attention in the only way I can .... more attention-getters are on the way. On May 7, 2002, the FBI and Nevada state authorities arrested Luke Helder, a 21-year-old college student, who confessed to being responsible for all of the bombs.
motive: The attacks were meant to bring attention to the perpetrator's antigovernment sentiment.

summary: Donald Jamesbrown Henry  fatally stabbed a man during a political discussion about former President Donald Trump in a home.

motive: Anti-Pelosi

motive: Trump supporter who thought that the Mar-a-Lago search was a call to arms

The assailant, identified as former Clark County official Robert Telles, was motivated by German’s reporting on misconduct and a personal affair tied to Telles’s office, which had contributed to Telles losing his reelection bid. Telles was later arrested, tried, and sentenced to prison for the murder.
motive: Mad about news stories reporter wrote

summary: Four white men in a pickup truck approached three Black teens outside a rural North Carolina home and asked whether they liked Donald Trump. When the teens said no, the men shot at them with a BB gun, hitting two and damaging the house, a shed, and a car.
motive: Pro-Trump

summary: At a campaign rally in Butler, Pennsylvania, Donald Trump was grazed in the upper right ear by gunfire by Thomas Matthew Crooks who fired from a nearby rooftop with an AR-15-style rifle. Crooks killed one rallygoer, critically injured two others, and was shot dead by a U.S. Secret Service counter-sniper

A 60‑year‑old man, Jeffrey Michael Kelly, was arrested after allegedly firing shots and BB pellets at a field office of the Democratic National Committee in Tempe, Arizona on multiple occasions. After the arrest, authorities say they found over 120 guns, 250,000 rounds of ammunition, body armor, silencers and a machine gun in his home.
motive: Anti-Democrats/Harris

motive: Anti-Trump

summary: An assailant with an SKS-style rifle was found hiding in shrubbery just outside the fence of Trump International Golf Club in West Palm Beach, Florida, while Donald Trump was playing golf. motive: Anti-Trump

summary: An assailant shot and decapitated his father inside their home in Levittown, Pennsylvania, United States, and then posted a video of the severed head on YouTube. The assailant, identified as Justin D. Mohn, claimed he was conducting a “citizen’s arrest” for alleged treason and said he feared being overpowered before the killing. Mohn stated that the beheading was intended as a political “message” to federal employees, urging mass resignations and other concessions.
motive: Anti-federal officials, anti-Biden

summary: A coordinated ambush occurred outside the Prairieland Detention Center (an U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement‑contracted facility) in Alvarado, Texas, where a group dressed in black military‑style clothing used fireworks and graffiti to lure officers, and then opened fire — wounding an Alvarado police officer who was shot in the neck.
motive: To protest ICE

summary: A 24-year-old man from James Island, South Carolina, named Daniel Clarke‑Pounder, has been charged in federal court for arson of a Tesla charging station in North Charleston on March 7.  Prosecutors allege he spray-painted political statements (“F*** Trump” and “Long Live Ukraine”) and then threw incendiary devices (Molotov cocktails) at the chargers, causing damage.
motive: Anti-Trump, Pro-Ukraine

summary: Two Democratic Minnesota lawmakers and their spouses were shot in coordinated attacks at their homes, one pair killed and the other wounded. Officials say the suspect, Vance Boelter, used a fake police car and had a list of Democratic and abortion-rights figures, calling it targeted political violence.
motive: Anti-Democrats

summary: On September 10, 2025, conservative activist Charlie Kirk was shot while speaking at an outdoor event at Utah Valley University in Orem, Utah. Investigators recovered the rifle used and messages from Tyler Robinson (the suspect) indicating he had planned the attack.
motive: Didn't like Kirk

summary: Patrick Joseph White drove to the CDC headquarters in Atlanta and opened fire, firing hundreds of rounds and killing an on‑duty police officer before taking his own life. motive: Unhappy with Covid-19 vaccines

summary: On January 1, 2025, a Tesla Cybertruck exploded outside the entrance of Trump International Hotel Las Vegas in Las Vegas, Nevada, killing the sole occupant and injuring seven bystanders. motive: To protest ICE

summary: 8/20/1970: Stuart W. Baker Jr. and John Jefferson Rimes firebombed a Selective Service Office located in a Masonic Temple in Reisterstown, Maryland, United States.
